response.justification: While this candidate demonstrates exceptional software engineering fundamentals, architectural thinking, and communication skills, they lack the required expertise in Java/Kotlin, Spring ecosystem, enterprise frameworks, build tools, and IDE internals. Their 15-year career trajectory has focused on web/backend services (TypeScript, Node.js, D language) rather than JVM tooling. The only IDE-related experience (LabWindows/CVI in 2014) is over a decade old, specialized to C, and outdated for this role's requirements.
strengths: Master of software craftsmanship: TDD, clean architecture, refactoring—demonstrated through multiple technical workshops, open-source libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial test runner), and consistent application across roles.
Proven communication and knowledge-sharing: Created full-day workshops, delivered FOSS4G talks, organized refactoring dojos, and maintains several well-architected open-source projects.
Strong architectural thinking: Successfully designed extensible systems (GISCollective platform, trial test runner) and evolved complex codebases with clean, maintainable design patterns.
concerns: No Java/Kotlin expertise: Resume mentions Java briefly (robotic arms, device orchestration) but shows no Kotlin experience, no depth of JVM understanding, and no enterprise Java applications.
Zero Spring/enterprise framework experience: No mention of Spring, Spring Boot, Quarkus, Hibernate, Maven, Gradle, or application server integration—core to the role.

No IDE or language tooling background: Only LabWindows/CVI experience (2014, for C, brief); recent roles (Google, Meta) focus on full-stack web and AI infrastructure, not IDE internals or developer tooling.

key_factors.factor: Missing Spring and enterprise framework experience
